# Eidos

> Eidos is a declarative offline-first toolkit for the web. `resource()` and `action()` replace
> hand-written Service Worker registration, Workbox cache configs, IndexedDB schemas, and retry
> queue logic. Works with React, Next.js, Svelte, Vue, and React Native.

Eidos generates a Service Worker, sets up cache strategies (cache-first, network-first,
stale-while-revalidate), and queues failed mutations for replay when the network returns —
all from a small declarative config.

## Docs

- [Quickstart](https://sweidos.vercel.app/docs/quickstart): install, setup, and first resource
- [API Reference](https://sweidos.vercel.app/docs/api): full `resource()` / `action()` API
- [Hooks](https://sweidos.vercel.app/docs/hooks): React hooks (`useResource`, `useAction`, `useEidosStatus`, etc.)
- [Examples](https://sweidos.vercel.app/docs/examples): common patterns and recipes
- [Advanced](https://sweidos.vercel.app/docs/advanced): conflict resolution, cancellation, replay locks
- [References](https://sweidos.vercel.app/docs/references): related concepts and further reading

## Playground

- [Overview](https://sweidos.vercel.app/overview): live offline-first demo
- [Resources](https://sweidos.vercel.app/resources): inspect cached resources
- [Actions](https://sweidos.vercel.app/actions): inspect the offline action queue
- [Inspector](https://sweidos.vercel.app/inspector): Service Worker / cache inspector

## Source

- [GitHub](https://github.com/iamadi11/eidos)
- [npm package](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@sweidos/eidos)
